results.possibleIdentity: Marilyn Perlman was an artist active in the late 1960s, 1970s, and 1980s. She is documented in the archives of the Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) as an artist associated with the Art Lending Service (ALS) and Art Advisory Service (AAS), with records specifically noting her involvement between 1973 and 1974. Her work was potentially made available for rent or sale through these services, which aimed to promote contemporary artists to collectors and corporate clients. In 1985, an exhibition of her work titled "Continuous Heavens" was held from June 29 to August 18; promotional materials and invitations for this exhibition are preserved within the archive of the prominent art critic and curator Nicolas Calas and his wife Elena Calas. This connection suggests she may have been part of the experimental or contemporary art circles in New York that Calas frequented, which often overlapped with organizations like Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.).
